feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: >Rachel Getting Married is a 2008 American drama film. It stars Anne Hathaway as…
entry.content: >Rachel Getting Married is a 2008 American drama film. It stars Anne Hathaway as a young woman temporarily released from rehab who returns home for the weekend for her sister's wedding Can we agree Kym was not the problem here?

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: No monsters. No music.
entry.content: I love playing Doom without monsters or music. Just exploring the strange maps and solving puzzles is the one of the most liminal things I've experienced in gaming. You really notice how weird and dreamlike the structures and environments are like this.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Obsidian is also in negotiations with Xbox to avoid closure
entry.content: >Today, the mood is different. Xbox is looking to reallocate where it spends money. The company wants to invest in its big franchises, and to do that, it needs to cut from elsewhere. Studios such as Compulsion, Ninja Theory, Double Fine, Obsidian, Undead Labs and others are deep in negotiation with Microsoft to avoid closure. The impact of this will likely reverberate across the business. >https://www.thegamebusiness.com/p/ xbox-divided-over-its-future-and?tr iedRedirect=true

They just look different than contin

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Guys, I genuinely think video games may actually die and the point is that we al…
entry.content: Guys, I genuinely think video games may actually die and the point is that we all get pushed into social media and AI for our entertainment options. It's an intentional push. Video games use too many resources and big tech wants all of those resources for AI and monitoring tech. Media streaming will be next.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Why is House MD the only live action adaptation of Sherlock Holmes that understa…
entry.content: Why is House MD the only live action adaptation of Sherlock Holmes that understands his character? He’s a highly observant antisocial freak who only cares about his own mental stimulation. The only reason he’s a detective is because in Victorian England it’s probably the most mentally stimulating job available. If you put him in the modern day with the amount of forensic equipment modern police have he’d probably rather be a murderer because of how much harder and stimulating it is. You can also

Open to educate, but it's far more likely they and others are like 18 and not in a place to be educated right now. They "like" this music for ulterior motives, and i

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Do i have government malware on my PC?
entry.content: I live in Australia and if i try to use utorrent suddenly my wifi stops working? i can do whatever i want, say whatever i want and nothing, But if i use utorrent suddenly my wifi stops working and the only fix is turning my PC off and on again. Also microsoft processes are taking up like 80% of CPU usage if i hit ctrl alt delete and bring up task manager but as soon as i do that it drops off? pic related best i could catch on it in task manager before it drops off. I feel like this is the govern

entry.title: /g/ Technology: >Your ISP/VPN provider sees your entire browsing history. >As soon as you use an…
entry.content: >Your ISP/VPN provider sees your entire browsing history. >As soon as you use an account-based service (Amazon, YouTube, X, TikTok, etc.), you are tracked 100% of the time, regardless of whether the app is sandboxed or not. >Installed apps can easily detect GrapheneOS, making your device's fingerprint highly unique within their networks due to the small user base. entry.title: /news/ News: OPSEC DEMONS in the US Military
entry.content: Del Cameron - May 20, 2026 https://www.wired.com/story/the-pen tagon-knew-enemies-could-track-troo ps-phones-for-years-now-they-are/ For nearly a decade, the Pentagon was warned—by its own contractors, analysts, and intelligence agencies—that anyone with a credit card could buy a map of where American troops sleep, work, and store nuclear weapons. Now the bill has come due in a war zone. A newly disclosed letter shows the warnings went unheeded: US Central Command now confirms it has received “m

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Why is Linux so fucking shit at handling audio over bluetooth? Half of the time …
entry.content: Why is Linux so fucking shit at handling audio over bluetooth? Half of the time my headsets will connect in shitty handsfree mode with no option to switch to high quality sound, or connect but have no sound and I have to manually switch to them. I've already tried 50 "solutions" I found online that do nothing so I'm resigned to rolling the dice everytime I connect my headphones.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: What makes Claude so much more accurate than other models when it comes to codin…
entry.content: What makes Claude so much more accurate than other models when it comes to coding? I tried many other models and none come close when it comes to coding. Most of them will lose track in complex project and end up shitting garbage code. Claude not only stays on track but ill also add useful feature that i didn't ask for.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Why you keep saying Xorg is not maintained anymore? They won't add new features,…

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Modern AI systems such as large language models are fundamentally probabilistic …
entry.content: Modern AI systems such as large language models are fundamentally probabilistic rather than inherently deterministic: at each step, they estimate a probability distribution over possible next outputs and choose from those probabilities. When randomness (sampling) is enabled, the same prompt can produce different valid responses on different runs. However, AI can be made effectively deterministic by fixing the model, inputs, random seed, and decoding settings (for example, using greedy decoding o

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: Crabs
entry.content: Those goddamn crabs in a bucket at work... They saw me using my private laptop during work all the time and they snitched to my boss. Now my boss pulled me to a meeting room for a "feedback meeting" and told me to only use my private laptop during breaks. And she also said that I get distracted easily when she's explaining stuff to me, like, that's just my neurodivergency wtf. That's just how I'm fucking wired. I'm not happy about this. :( almost made me cry

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: The game industry is making me incredibly depressed and I'm done
entry.content: Everything coming out this past month from Sony, Microsoft, Steam, GTA, Bethesda, Bungie, EVERYTHING has been a daily dose of end stage capitalism. Game developers deserve to have jobs, and youre not entitled to ruin their lives by firing them just because you now own a company. Nothing about the games industry has me excited and I'm happy I got a PS5, Xbox X, Xbox S and Nintendo Switch because I am not buying another console again. I'm not a mindless consumer. This industry is on the fast track

entry.content: Somebody's leaning on them. You don't just torpedo half your business model and make yourself the center of public ridicule for the fun of it. Somebody's leaning on them to do this. My guess is Larry Fink but it's equally possible it could be somebody from State Street or Vanguard.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: The Imitation Game is a great film
entry.content: Benedict Cumberbatch does a brilliant job of highlighting the evils of bigotry and religious opposition to scientific progress. What the world needs right now is to replace all of these old useless Christian feast days and celebrations with days dedicated to the saints of gay rights and liberation: Harvey Milk, Oscar Wilde, Alan Turing, Freddie Mercury, etc. These are the new saints. Let's celebrate "Michel Foucault Day" instead of Easter Sunday. >>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n uPZUUED5uk

entry.title: /g/ Technology: >apt install --no-install-recommends A >Installing dependencies: B C >apt autore…
entry.content: >apt install --no-install-recommends A >Installing dependencies: B C >apt autoremove --purge A >Removing dependencies: B After installing A, C become recommended dependency for previously installed package D, thus apt autoremove will not remove it 33 years of development btw

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: I'm enjoying watching the entire gaming industry crash and burn. We're only half…
entry.content: I'm enjoying watching the entire gaming industry crash and burn. We're only halfway through 2026. Who knows what the next six months will bring? I'm not even thinking about what 2027 will be like. Isn't it exciting? You keep thinking "Well, it can't get any worse." But it always can get worse. More price hikes? More layoffs? PS6 and Helix will probably cost like $1,300, but at least you still have 2027 to buy physical PlayStation games. Nintendo basically already said fuck physical, and now Play
